title: Ticketly Backlog
description: A generated backlog: a company, a project, and its epics and tickets. Single source of truth for every Ticketly renderer and validator.

company.description: Company / organization this backlog is for. The user MUST supply this; Ticketly never invents it. Use the real name the user provides.

project.description: Project name this backlog belongs to, supplied by the user, e.g. 'Demo Project'.

id.description: Epics: EPIC-<PREFIX> (e.g. EPIC-ARC). Tasks: <PREFIX>-NNN (e.g. ARC-001). Prefix is the epic theme; children inherit it.

title.description: Short task name, e.g. 'Backup restore and rollback drill'.

type.description: Epic groups child tickets; Task is a unit of work.

parent.description: For Tasks: the EPIC-<PREFIX> id this belongs to. For Epics: null.

effort.description: Fibonacci story points. Epics use 0 (sized via their children).

dependencies.description: Ids of tickets that must be done first. Empty array if none.

description.description: Clear statement of intent / what and why.

acceptance_criteria.description: Testable, closeable conditions. Required (non-empty) for Tasks; may be empty for Epics.

needs_clarification.description: Guardrail: true when requirements were too thin and scope was NOT invented. Flag instead of hallucinating.

assignee.description: Optional. Person responsible, or null/unassigned. Hidden by default for small/solo teams.

due_date.description: Optional ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) or null. Hidden by default.
